card.intuition: Hydration is the process of attaching event listeners to server-rendered HTML. Islands Architecture goes a step further by only hydrating small, interactive parts of the page (Islands) while keeping the rest as static HTML.
card.analogy: A desert with small oases: the desert (Static HTML) is large and dry (no JS). The oases (Islands) are small pockets where life (interactivity/JS) exists. You don't need to water the whole desert just for the oases to thrive.
when_to_use: Improving 'Total Blocking Time' (TBT) by reducing the amount of JS that runs on page load
Mixed-content pages where only 10% of the UI needs state
gotchas: Hydration Mismatch: if the HTML from the server doesn't perfectly match what the client expects, you'll get a nasty warning and potentially flickering UI
Pure 'Islands' can't easily share state with each other unless you use a global store or custom events

exercise.prompt: Identify the process of making static HTML interactive in the browser.
exercise.starter_code:
def make_interactive(html):
    # Browser takes the HTML and 'adds life' to it
